name: fastavro (Fast Avro Serialization for Python)

performance.notes: Cython implementation; 10-100x faster than official avro-python3

agent_readiness.notes: Fast Avro implementation for Python. import fastavro. schema = {'type': 'record', 'name': 'User', 'fields': [{'name': 'id', 'type': 'long'}, {'name': 'name', 'type': 'string'}]}. with open('data.avro', 'wb') as f: fastavro.writer(f, schema, records). with open('data.avro', 'rb') as f: reader = fastavro.reader(f). for record in reader: print(record). Parsed schema: parsed = fastavro.parse_schema(schema). schemaless_writer / schemaless_reader for Kafka without file header. Type annotations. Confluent Schema Registry compatible.

gotchas: Schema must be parsed before serialization for best performance: parse_schema()
schemaless_writer/reader needed for Kafka (no file header)
Schema evolution rules still require careful design
best_when: Kafka pipelines using Avro serialization (preferred over official library)
avoid_when: Non-Avro use cases (use msgpack or cbor2 instead)
When protobuf schema evolution control is preferred
summary: fastavro is a fast Cython implementation of Apache Avro — 10-100x faster than official avro-python3. fastavro.writer(f, schema, records) / fastavro.reader(f). parse_schema() for performance. schemaless_writer/reader for Kafka. Type annotations. MIT license. The recommended Avro library for Python — always use fastavro over avro-python3. Best for Kafka pipelines and Hadoop ecosystem.
